title: populus nigra subsp. monilifera
intro: Populus nigra subsp. monilifera, also known as Black Cottonwood or Black Poplar, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The bark and leaves of the Black Cottonwood have been used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are said to provide numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. The herbal preparations made from Populus nigra subsp. monilifera are believed to have a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and promoting wound healing.
